prompt.name: pattern-copy-play

content.thinking:
Let me analyze these instructions carefully. I need to:

1. Analyze the codebase thoroughly
2. Build a default app environment in preview.tsx
3. Support portals with preview-body.html
4. Mock side effects globally with MSW
5. Write stories for ~10 components
6. Write play functions for every story
7. Include a CssCheck story
8. Cover real patterns
9. Verify with Vitest and TypeScript

Let me start by exploring the codebase structure.

content.text: I'll start by analyzing the codebase thoroughly. Let me explore the project structure and key files in parallel.

input.prompt:
Thoroughly explore this codebase to understand:
1. The full project structure (directories, key files)
2. The entry point (main.tsx or index.tsx)
3. App.tsx and its provider tree
4. All providers/contexts used
5. All CSS/style imports at the root level
6. Router setup and pages
7. Any portal usage (createPortal, modal roots, etc.)
8. Data fetching patterns (fetch, axios, react-query, etc.)
9. Browser state usage (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies)
10. Key reusable components

Start from the root, then trace imports from main.tsx -> App.tsx -> pages -> components. Be very thorough - read actual file contents, not just list files. I need to understand every provider, every global CSS import, every data fetching pattern, and every browser state read.

1→import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'

     6→import { store } from './app-state'

    11→export default function App() {

    18→        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
    19→          <GlobalStyle />
    20→          <AppRoutes />
    21→        </ThemeProvider>
    22→      </StoreProvider>
    23→    </Router>
    24→  )
    25→}

1→import { useLayoutEffect } from 'react'

    11→export const AppRoutes: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<unknown>> = ({ children }) => {
    12→  const location = useLocation()
    13→  // Scroll to top when a path changes
    14→  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    15→    window.scrollTo(0, 0)
    16→  }, [location.pathname])
    17→
    18→  return (
    19→    <>
    20→      <div id="modal" />
    21→      <Routes>
    22→        <Route path="/categories" element={<CategoryListPage />} />
    23→        <Route path="/categories/:id" element={<CategoryDetailPage />} />
    24→        <Route path="/restaurants/:id" element={<RestaurantDetailPage />} />
    25→        <Route path="/checkout" element={<CheckoutPage />} />
    26→        <Route path="/success" element={<SuccessPage />} />
    27→        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
    28→        {children}
    29→      </Routes>
    30→    </>
    31→  )
    32→}

1→import { createSlice, CaseReducer, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→import { CartItem } from '../cart'
     4→
     5→export type OrderState = {
     6→  items: CartItem[]
     7→}
     8→
     9→const saveOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState, PayloadAction<CartItem[]>> = (state, action) => {
    10→  state.items = action.payload
    11→}
    12→
    13→const clearOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState> = (state) => {
    14→  state.items = []
    15→}
    16→
    17→const initialState: OrderState = {
    18→  items: [],
    19→}
    20→
    21→const orderSlice = createSlice({
    22→  name: 'order',
    23→  initialState,
    24→  reducers: {
    25→    saveOrderAction: saveOrder,
    26→    clearOrderAction: clearOrder,
    27→  },
    28→})
    29→
    30→// Extract the action creators object and the reducer
    31→export const { actions, reducer } = orderSlice
    32→
    33→// Extract and export each action creator by name
    34→export const { saveOrderAction, clearOrderAction } = actions
    35→
    36→// Export the reducer, either as a default or named export
    37→export default reducer
    38→

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import { CSSTransition } from 'react-transition-group'
     3→
     4→import { Portal } from '../Portal'
     5→import { useKey } from '../../hooks/useKeyboard'
     6→import { Button } from '../Button'
     7→import { useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks/useBodyScrollLock'
     8→
     9→import { ModalContent, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Modal.styles'
    10→
    11→type ModalProps = {
    12→  isOpen: boolean
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    23→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    24→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    25→
    26→  return (
    27→    <Portal selector="#modal">
    28→      <CSSTransition
    29→        in={isOpen}
    30→        timeout={300}
    31→        classNames="modal"
    32→        unmountOnExit
    33→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    34→      >
    35→        <ModalContent data-testid="modal" ref={contentRef}>
    36→          <TopBar>
    37→            <Button
    38→              data-testid="modal-close-btn"
    39→              onClick={onClose}
    40→              clear
    41→              round
    42→              icon="cross"
    43→              aria-label="close modal"
    44→              iconSize={16}
    45→            />
    46→          </TopBar>
    47→          {children}
    48→        </ModalContent>
    49→      </CSSTransition>
    50→
    51→      <CSSTransition
    52→        in={isOpen}
    53→        timeout={300}
    54→        classNames="backdrop"
    55→        unmountOnExit
    56→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    57→      >
    58→        <Backdrop data-testid="modal-backdrop" ref={backdropRef} onClick={onClose} />
    59→      </CSSTransition>
    60→    </Portal>
    61→  )
    62→}
    63→

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→export const useKey = (key: string, keyDownCb = () => {}, keyUpCb = () => {}): boolean => {
     4→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false)
     5→  const match = useCallback((event: any) => key.toLowerCase() === event.key.toLowerCase(), [key])
     6→
     7→  const onKeyDown = useCallback(
     8→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
     9→      if (match(event)) {
    10→        setPressed(true)
    11→        keyDownCb()
    12→      }
    13→    },
    14→    [keyDownCb, match]
    15→  )
    16→
    17→  const onKeyUp = useCallback(
    18→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
    19→      if (match(event)) {
    20→        setPressed(false)
    21→        keyUpCb()
    22→      }
    23→    },
    24→    [keyUpCb, match]
    25→  )
    26→
    27→  useEffect(() => {
    28→    globalThis.addEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    29→    globalThis.addEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    30→
    31→    return () => {
    32→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    33→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    34→    }
    35→  }, [key, onKeyDown, onKeyUp])
    36→
    37→  return pressed
    38→}

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import { CSSTransition } from 'react-transition-group'
     3→
     4→import { useKey, useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks'
     5→import { Button } from '../Button'
     6→import { Heading } from '../typography'
     7→
     8→import { SidebarContainer, SidebarContent, SidebarFooter, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Sidebar.styles'
     9→
    10→type SidebarProps = {
    11→  isOpen: boolean
    12→  title: string
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→  footer?: React.ReactNode
    15→}
    16→
    17→export const Sidebar: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<SidebarProps>> = ({
    18→  children,
    19→  footer,
    20→  isOpen,
    21→  title,
    22→  onClose,
    23→}) => {
    24→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    25→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    26→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    27→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    28→
    29→  return (
    30→    <>
    31→      <CSSTransition
    32→        in={isOpen}
    33→        timeout={300}
    34→        classNames="sidebar"
    35→        unmountOnExit
    36→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    37→      >
    38→        <SidebarContainer data-testid="sidebar" ref={contentRef}>
    39→          <TopBar>
    40→            <Heading level={4}>{title}</Heading>
    41→            <Button
    42→              aria-label="close sidebar"
    43→              data-testid="sidebar-close-btn"
    44→              onClick={onClose}
    45→              clear
    46→              round
    47→              icon="cross"
    48→              iconSize={16}
    49→            />
    50→          </TopBar>
    51→          <SidebarContent data-testid="sidebar-content">{children}</SidebarContent>
    52→          {footer && <SidebarFooter data-testid="sidebar-footer">{footer}</SidebarFooter>}
    53→        </SidebarContainer>
    54→      </CSSTransition>
    55→
    56→      <CSSTransition
    57→        in={isOpen}
    58→        timeout={300}
    59→        classNames="backdrop"
    60→        unmountOnExit
    61→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    62→      >
    63→        <Backdrop data-testid="Sidebar-backdrop" onClick={onClose} />
    64→      </CSSTransition>
    65→    </>
    66→  )
    67→}

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me provide a thorough summary of the codebase exploration:

## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis

### 1. Project Structure

This is a **food delivery/restaurant app (MealDrop)** built with React, TypeScript, and Vite. The project has a clear directory organization:

- `/src` - Source code
- `/public` - Static assets (sprite-map.svg, images)
- `.storybook` - Storybook configuration
- `node_modules` - Dependencies

### 2. Entry Point

**Main file: `/src/index.tsx`**
- Uses React 18's `createRoot` API
- Mounts app into `#root` element
- Wraps App component with StrictMode
- Registers service worker (currently unregistered)

### 3. App.tsx & Provider Tree

**File: `/src/App.tsx`**

The application uses a complete provider stack:

```
<StrictMode>
  <Router> (BrowserRouter)
    <StoreProvider> (Redux)
      <ThemeProvider> (styled-components)
        <GlobalStyle />
        <AppRoutes />
      </ThemeProvider>
    </StoreProvider>
  </Router>
</StrictMode>
```

**Providers Used:**
1. **React Router** - `BrowserRouter` for navigation
2. **Redux** - State management via `react-redux` Provider
3. **Styled Components** - `ThemeProvider` for theming
4. **Dark Mode** - `use-dark-mode` hook (controlled at App level)

### 4. All Providers/Contexts

**Redux Store** (`/src/app-state/store.ts`):
- Combines two slices: `cart` and `order`
- Exports typed hooks: `useAppDispatch` and `useAppSelector`

**Redux Slices:**

**Cart Slice** (`/src/app-state/cart/cart.ts`):
- State: `{ visible: boolean, items: CartItem[] }`
- Actions: `toggleVisibilityAction`, `saveItemAction`, `clearItemAction`, `removeItemAction`, `clearCartAction`
- Selectors: `selectCartVisibility`, `selectCartItems`, `selectCartItemsCount`, `selectCartTotal`

**Order Slice** (`/src/app-state/order/order.ts`):
- State: `{ items: CartItem[] }`
- Actions: `saveOrderAction`, `clearOrderAction`
- Selectors: `selectOrderItems`, `selectOrderTotal`

**Dark Mode:**
- Managed by `use-dark-mode` hook at App level
- Reads/writes to `globalThis.window`
- Switches between `lightTheme` and `darkTheme`

### 5. All CSS/Style Imports at Root

**Global Styles** (`/src/styles/GlobalStyle.tsx`):
- Imports: `react-multi-carousel/lib/styles.css`, `react-loading-skeleton/dist/skeleton.css`
- Defines Montserrat (400, 500, 700, 900) and Hind (400, 500) fonts from Google Fonts
- Includes CSS reset via `resetCSS`
- Smooth transitions for dark mode (250ms)
- Global classes: `.container`, `.container-desktop`, `.bolder`, `.copyright`, `.footer-bottom`
- Carousel overrides for react-multi-carousel

**CSS Reset** (`/src/styles/CSSReset.ts`):
- Normalizes form elements
- Resets default styles on buttons, inputs, selects, textareas
- Fixes SVG overflow
- Sets sensible defaults for positioning

**Theme System** (`/src/styles/theme.ts`):
- **Light Theme** - Default
- **Dark Theme** - Full override of colors
- Uses styled-components `DefaultTheme` interface
- Color palette: baseColors with white, black, red, grey, green, blue variants
- Spacing units based on em (0.25em to 5.25em)
- Border radius: xs(4px) to xxl(40px), round(50%)
- Typography: 4 font sizes, 4 font weights
- Box shadows: card, inner, outerBorder

**Breakpoints** (`/src/styles/breakpoints.ts`):
- XS: 400px, S: 640px, M: 768px, L: 1024px, XL: 1440px

### 6. Router Setup & Pages

**Routes File** (`/src/Routes.tsx`):
- Scrolls to top on route change
- Creates `#modal` div for portals
- Routes:
  - `/` → HomePage
  - `/categories` → CategoryListPage
  - `/categories/:id` → CategoryDetailPage
  - `/restaurants/:id` → RestaurantDetailPage
  - `/checkout` → CheckoutPage
  - `/success` → SuccessPage

**Pages:**

1. **HomePage** (`/src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.tsx`)
   - Uses PageTemplate (default type)
   - Components: Banner, RestaurantsSection, AwardWinningSection, CategoriesSection

2. **CategoryListPage** (`/src/pages/CategoryListPage/CategoryListPage.tsx`)
   - Lists all food categories
   - Static data from `/stub/categories.ts`

3. **CategoryDetailPage** (`/src/pages/CategoryDetailPage/CategoryDetailPage.tsx`)
   - Fetches restaurants by category ID
   - Shows loading skeletons during fetch
   - Error handling for empty results

4. **RestaurantDetailPage** (`/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.tsx`)
   - Fetches single restaurant by ID
   - Shows menu items (food, dessert, drinks)
   - Modal for adding items to cart
   - Redux dispatch: `saveItemAction`, `clearItemAction`

5. **CheckoutPage** (`/src/pages/CheckoutPage/CheckoutPage.tsx`)
   - Multi-step form (Contact Details, Delivery Details)
   - Order summary sidebar
   - Uses Redux: `selectCartItems`

6. **SuccessPage** (`/src/pages/SuccessPage/SuccessPage.tsx`)
   - Shows order confirmation
   - Uses Redux: `selectOrderItems`

**PageTemplate** (`/src/templates/PageTemplate.tsx`):
- Types: `default`, `sticky-header`, `basic`
- Default: Header + Container + Footer
- Sticky-header: Sticky Header + Container + Footer
- Basic: Logo-only Header + Container (no footer)

### 7. Portal Usage

**Portal Component** (`/src/components/Portal.tsx`):
- Uses `createPortal` from react-dom
- Takes selector prop to find target DOM element
- Used for modals and sidebars

**Modal Portal Target:**
- Created in Routes.tsx: `<div id="modal" />`
- Used by Modal component via `<Portal selector="#modal">`

**Modal Component** (`/src/components/Modal/Modal.tsx`):
- Uses Portal to render in #modal
- CSSTransition for animations (300ms)
- Keyboard support: Escape key closes
- Body scroll lock when open
- Backdrop click closes

**Sidebar Component** (`/src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.tsx`):
- Similar animation and keyboard support
- Used by ShoppingCartMenu
- No explicit portal (renders inline with backdrop)

### 8. Data Fetching Patterns

**API Layer** (`/src/api/index.ts` and `/src/api/conditional-logic.ts`):

**Base URL:** `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants`

**API Methods:**
- `getRestaurants()` - Fetch all restaurants
- `getRestaurantById(id)` - Fetch single restaurant
- `getRestaurantsByCategory(category)` - Fetch by category

**Technology:** Axios with caching
- In-memory Map cache
- Cache disabled in test/Storybook environments
- Returns typed AxiosResponse

**Data Fetching Hooks** (`/src/api/hooks.ts`):
- `useFetchRestaurants()` - Returns { status, restaurants, retryRequest }
- `useFetchRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)` - Returns { status, restaurants, retryRequest }
- `useFetchRestaurant(id)` - Returns { status, restaurant, retryRequest }

**Status Values:**
- `idle`, `loading`, `success`
- Error status codes: `404`, `500` (error?.response?.status || 500)

**Conditional Logic** (`/src/api/conditional-logic.ts`):
- Mocked API for Storybook and test environments
- Uses data from `/src/stub/restaurants.ts`
- Real API for production

### 9. Browser State Usage

**NO localStorage or sessionStorage found.**

**Browser State Used:**
- **Dark Mode State:** via `use-dark-mode` hook (reads/writes to window object internally)
- **Document manipulation:** 
  - Body scroll lock: `document.body.style.overflow`
  - Portal element selection: `document.querySelector()`
- **Navigation State:** Managed by React Router (useNavigate, useParams)
- **Keyboard Events:** Global event listeners (useKey hook)

### 10. Key Reusable Components

**Layout Components:**
- `PageTemplate` - Page wrapper with header/footer variants
- `Header` + `HeaderComponent` - Top navigation with cart, theme toggle
- `Footer` - Footer with links
- `Sidebar` - Animated sidebar panel
- `Modal` - Centered modal with animations

**Data Display:**
- `RestaurantCard` + `RestaurantCardSkeleton` - Restaurant preview cards
- `Review` - Star rating display
- `Badge` - Small tag badges
- `Category` - Category card

**Forms:**
- `Input` - Text input field
- `Select` - Dropdown select
- `MultiStepForm` - Multi-step checkout form
- `ContactDetails` - Form step 1
- `DeliveryDetails` - Form step 2

**Shopping Cart:**
- `ShoppingCartMenu` - Sidebar menu with cart items
- `ShoppingCartItem` - Individual cart item in menu
- `OrderSummary` - Order summary display
- `FoodItemModal` - Add-to-cart modal for food items

**Typography:**
- `Heading` - Heading component (h1-h4)
- `Body` - Body text component

**Icons & Images:**
- `Icon` - SVG icon from sprite-map.svg
- `Logo` - App logo
- `AnimatedIllustration` - Animated illustrations
- `TopBanner` - Page header banners

**Navigation:**
- `Breadcrumb` - Breadcrumb navigation

**Utilities:**
- `Portal` - Portal to render in different DOM location
- `Button` - Primary CTA button with variants
- `IconButton` - Icon-only button
- `Spinner` - Loading spinner
- `ErrorBlock` - Error message display
- `PageSection` - Section container with title

**Cart Operations** (Redux actions dispatched):
- Save/add item: `saveItemAction(cartItem)`
- Remove item: `removeItemAction(cartItem)`
- Clear item: `clearItemAction(cartItem)`
- Toggle visibility: `toggleVisibilityAction()`
- Save order: `saveOrderAction(cartItems)`
- Clear order: `clearOrderAction()`

**Helper Functions** (`/src/helpers/index.ts`):
- `toCurrency(number)` - Format price with locale currency
- `isMobile()` - Check if user agent is mobile (regex: /Mobi/i)
- `getCurrency()` - Get locale currency code

### 11. Key Files Summary

**State Management:**
- `/src/app-state/store.ts` - Redux store config
- `/src/app-state/cart/cart.ts` - Cart slice
- `/src/app-state/order/order.ts` - Order slice
- `/src/app-state/hooks.ts` - Typed Redux hooks

**Styling:**
- `/src/styles/theme.ts` - Theme definitions
- `/src/styles/GlobalStyle.tsx` - Global styles
- `/src/styles/CSSReset.ts` - CSS reset
- `/src/styles/breakpoints.ts` - Responsive breakpoints

**Components:** 22+ reusable components in `/src/components/`

**Pages:** 6 pages in `/src/pages/`

**Hooks:**
- `/src/hooks/useKeyboard.ts` - Keyboard event listener
- `/src/hooks/useBodyScrollLock.ts` - Body scroll control

**API:**
- `/src/api/index.ts` - API client with axios
- `/src/api/hooks.ts` - Data fetching hooks
- `/src/api/conditional-logic.ts` - Mocked vs real API

**Stub Data:**
- `/src/stub/restaurants.ts` - Mock restaurant data
- `/src/stub/categories.ts` - Food categories
- `/src/stub/cart-items.ts` - Mock cart items

All files are thoroughly type-safe with TypeScript, using styled-components for styling, Redux Toolkit for state management, and React Router for navigation.

- `Category` - Category card

**Forms:**
- `Input` - Text input field
- `Select` - Dropdown select
- `MultiStepForm` - Multi-step checkout form
- `ContactDetails` - Form step 1
- `DeliveryDetails` - Form step 2

**Shopping Cart:**
- `ShoppingCartMenu` - Sidebar menu with cart items
- `ShoppingCartItem` - Individual cart item in menu
- `OrderSummary` - Order summary display
- `FoodItemModal` - Add-to-cart modal for food items

**Typography:**
- `Heading` - Heading component (h1-h4)
- `Body` - Body text component

**Icons & Images:**
- `Icon` - SVG icon from sprite-map.svg
- `Logo` - App logo
- `AnimatedIllustration` - Animated illustrations
- `TopBanner` - Page header banners

**Navigation:**
- `Breadcrumb` - Breadcrumb navigation

**Utilities:**
- `Portal` - Portal to render in different DOM location
- `Button` - Primary CTA button with variants
- `IconButton` - Icon-only button
- `Spinner` - Loading spinner
- `ErrorBlock` - Error message display
- `PageSection` - Section container with title

**Cart Operations** (Redux actions dispatched):
- Save/add item: `saveItemAction(cartItem)`
- Remove item: `removeItemAction(cartItem)`
- Clear item: `clearItemAction(cartItem)`
- Toggle visibility: `toggleVisibilityAction()`
- Save order: `saveOrderAction(cartItems)`
- Clear order: `clearOrderAction()`

**Helper Functions** (`/src/helpers/index.ts`):
- `toCurrency(number)` - Format price with locale currency
- `isMobile()` - Check if user agent is mobile (regex: /Mobi/i)
- `getCurrency()` - Get locale currency code

### 11. Key Files Summary

**State Management:**
- `/src/app-state/store.ts` - Redux store config
- `/src/app-state/cart/cart.ts` - Cart slice
- `/src/app-state/order/order.ts` - Order slice
- `/src/app-state/hooks.ts` - Typed Redux hooks

**Styling:**
- `/src/styles/theme.ts` - Theme definitions
- `/src/styles/GlobalStyle.tsx` - Global styles
- `/src/styles/CSSReset.ts` - CSS reset
- `/src/styles/breakpoints.ts` - Responsive breakpoints

**Components:** 22+ reusable components in `/src/components/`

**Pages:** 6 pages in `/src/pages/`

**Hooks:**
- `/src/hooks/useKeyboard.ts` - Keyboard event listener
- `/src/hooks/useBodyScrollLock.ts` - Body scroll control

**API:**
- `/src/api/index.ts` - API client with axios
- `/src/api/hooks.ts` - Data fetching hooks
- `/src/api/conditional-logic.ts` - Mocked vs real API

**Stub Data:**
- `/src/stub/restaurants.ts` - Mock restaurant data
- `/src/stub/categories.ts` - Food categories
- `/src/stub/cart-items.ts` - Mock cart items

All files are thoroughly type-safe with TypeScript, using styled-components for styling, Redux Toolkit for state management, and React Router for navigation.

import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}
   101→

file.content:
import { DefaultTheme } from 'styled-components'

declare module 'styled-components' {
  export interface DefaultTheme {
    name: 'light' | 'dark'
    fonts: {
      family: string
    }
    spacing: typeof spacing
    color: typeof color
    borderRadius: typeof borderRadius
    boxShadow: typeof boxShadow
    typography: typeof typography
  }
}

const baseColors = {
  white: '#FFFFFF ',
  black: '#202020',
  red: {
    base: '#D70808',
    light1: '#E57373',
    light2: '#EF9A9A',
    dark1: '#C62828',
    dark2: '#B71C1C',
  },
  otherBlack: '#2C2C2C ',
  grey: {
    base: '#909090',
    light1: '#A6A6A6',
    light2: '#BCBCBC',
    light3: '#D2D2D2',
    light4: '#E9E9E9',
    light5: '#F5F6F7',
    light6: '#F9F9F9',
    dark1: '#797979',
    dark2: '#636363',
    dark3: '#4D4D4D',
    dark4: '#363636',
    dark5: '#2C2C2C',
    dark6: '#202020',
  },
  green: {
    base: '#E5F8BC',
    light1: '#E8F9C3',
    light2: '#EAF9C9',
    light3: '#EDFAD0',
    light4: '#EFFBD7',
    light5: '#F2FCDE',
    dark1: '#CEDFA9',
    dark2: '#B7C696',
    dark3: '#A0AE84',
    dark4: '#899571',
    dark5: '#737C5E',
  },
  blue: {
    base: '#B1DDE4',
    light1: '#B9E0E7',
    light2: '#C1E4E9',
    light3: '#C8E7EC',
    light4: '#D0EBEF',
    light5: '#D8EEF2',
    dark1: '#9FC7CD',
    dark2: '#8EB1B6',
    dark3: '#7C9BA0',
    dark4: '#6A8589',
    dark5: '#596f72',
  },
}

const spaceUnit = 1

const borderRadius = {
  xs: '4px',
  s: '8px',
  m: '16px',
  l: '24px',
  xl: '32px',
  xxl: '40px',
  round: '50%',
}

const spacing = {
  xxs: `${0.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
  xs: `${0.5 * spaceUnit}em`,
  s: `${spaceUnit}em`,
  m: `${1.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
  l: `${2 * spaceUnit}em`,
  xl: `${3.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
  xxl: `${5.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
}

const boxShadow = {
  card: '0px 14px 26px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08)',
  inner: 'inset 0 3px 0 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',
  outerBorder: `0 0 0 1px ${baseColors.blue.dark2}, 0 0 0 4px ${baseColors.blue.light5}`,
}

const typography = {
  fontSize: {
    body: '1.125rem',
    bodyS: '1rem',
    bodyXS: '0.9rem',
    bodyXXS: '0.72rem',
    heading1: '2.74rem',
    heading2: '2.19rem',
    heading3: '1.75rem',
    heading4: '1.4rem',
  },
  fontWeight: {
    black: '900',
    bold: '700',
    medium: '500',
    regular: '400',
  },
}

const color = {
  accentText: baseColors.black,
  badgeBackground: baseColors.grey.light4,
  badgeText: baseColors.grey.dark2,
  cartButtonText: baseColors.grey.base,
  bannerBackground: baseColors.blue.base,
  buttonClear: 'transparent',
  buttonClearHover: baseColors.grey.light5,
  buttonPrimary: baseColors.grey.dark6,
  buttonPrimaryHover: baseColors.grey.dark5, // recheck
  buttonSecondary: baseColors.green.base,
  buttonSecondaryHover: baseColors.green.dark1, // recheck
  buttonText: baseColors.white,
  cardBackground: baseColors.grey.light6,
  checkoutTopBackground: baseColors.green.base,
  checkoutBottomBackground: baseColors.grey.light6,
  error: baseColors.red.dark2,
  formBackground: baseColors.white,
  foodItemBackground: baseColors.white,
  footerBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
  headerBackground: baseColors.white,
  restaurantDetailBackground: baseColors.white,
  headerBorder: baseColors.grey.light4,
  inputBackground: baseColors.grey.light5,
  inputHint: baseColors.grey.dark1,
  inputIcon: baseColors.grey.dark6,
  label: baseColors.grey.dark4,
  labelActive: baseColors.grey.dark6,
  menuSectionBackground: baseColors.grey.light6,
  stepsIndicatorInnerBar: baseColors.grey.dark6,
  stepsIndicatorOuterBar: baseColors.grey.light5,
  orderSummaryBackground: baseColors.white,
  overlayBackground: baseColors.white,
  overlayHeader: baseColors.grey.light5,
  primaryText: baseColors.otherBlack,
  secondaryText: baseColors.grey.dark2,
  // This is just used to demo a11y violations, use it in the Review component
  bannerText: baseColors.blue.dark3,
  reviewText: baseColors.blue.dark5,
  screenBackground: baseColors.white,
  sidebarHeader: baseColors.grey.dark4,
  sidebarFooter: baseColors.white,
  skeletonBase: baseColors.grey.light4,
  skeletonHighlight: baseColors.grey.light5,
  topBannerBackground: baseColors.green.base,
  newRestaurantTag: baseColors.green.base,
  white: baseColors.white,
  black: baseColors.black,
}

export const lightTheme: DefaultTheme = {
  borderRadius,
  boxShadow,
  color,
  fonts: {
    family: 'NunitoSans, sans-serif',
  },
  name: 'light',
  spacing,
  typography,
}

export const darkTheme: DefaultTheme = {
  ...lightTheme,
  boxShadow: {
    ...boxShadow,
    outerBorder: `0 0 0 2px ${baseColors.blue.dark5}, 0 0 0 4px ${baseColors.blue.base}`,
  },
  color: {
    ...lightTheme.color,
    badgeBackground: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    badgeText: baseColors.grey.light1,
    cartButtonText: baseColors.grey.dark2,
    bannerBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    buttonClearHover: baseColors.grey.dark4, // recheck
    buttonPrimary: baseColors.green.base,
    buttonPrimaryHover: baseColors.green.dark1, // recheck
    buttonSecondary: baseColors.green.base,
    buttonText: baseColors.otherBlack,
    cardBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    checkoutTopBackground: baseColors.grey.dark6,
    checkoutBottomBackground: baseColors.grey.dark1,
    formBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    foodItemBackground: baseColors.otherBlack,
    headerBackground: baseColors.grey.dark6,
    restaurantDetailBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    headerBorder: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    inputBackground: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    inputIcon: baseColors.white,
    inputHint: baseColors.grey.light1,
    label: baseColors.grey.light4,
    labelActive: baseColors.white,
    menuSectionBackground: baseColors.grey.dark6,
    stepsIndicatorInnerBar: baseColors.green.base,
    stepsIndicatorOuterBar: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    orderSummaryBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    overlayBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    overlayHeader: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    primaryText: baseColors.white,
    reviewText: baseColors.blue.base,
    screenBackground: baseColors.grey.dark6,
    sidebarHeader: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    sidebarFooter: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    skeletonBase: baseColors.grey.dark3,
    skeletonHighlight: baseColors.grey.dark2,
    topBannerBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
  },
  name: 'dark',
}

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import { CSSTransition } from 'react-transition-group'
     3→
     4→import { Portal } from '../Portal'
     5→import { useKey } from '../../hooks/useKeyboard'
     6→import { Button } from '../Button'
     7→import { useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks/useBodyScrollLock'
     8→
     9→import { ModalContent, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Modal.styles'
    10→
    11→type ModalProps = {
    12→  isOpen: boolean
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    23→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    24→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    25→
    26→  return (
    27→    <Portal selector="#modal">
    28→      <CSSTransition
    29→        in={isOpen}
    30→        timeout={300}
    31→        classNames="modal"
    32→        unmountOnExit
    33→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    34→      >
    35→        <ModalContent data-testid="modal" ref={contentRef}>
    36→          <TopBar>
    37→            <Button
    38→              data-testid="modal-close-btn"
    39→              onClick={onClose}
    40→              clear
    41→              round
    42→              icon="cross"
    43→              aria-label="close modal"
    44→              iconSize={16}
    45→            />
    46→          </TopBar>
    47→          {children}
    48→        </ModalContent>
    49→      </CSSTransition>
    50→
    51→      <CSSTransition
    52→        in={isOpen}
    53→        timeout={300}
    54→        classNames="backdrop"
    55→        unmountOnExit
    56→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    57→      >
    58→        <Backdrop data-testid="modal-backdrop" ref={backdropRef} onClick={onClose} />
    59→      </CSSTransition>
    60→    </Portal>
    61→  )
    62→}
    63→

import styled from 'styled-components'

import { PageTemplate } from '../../templates/PageTemplate'
import { categories } from '../../stub/categories'

import { RestaurantsSection } from './components/RestaurantsSection'
import { CategoriesSection } from './components/CategoriesSection/CategoriesSection'
import { Banner } from './components/Banner'
import { AwardWinningSection } from './components/AwardWinningSection'

const Spacing = styled.div`
  margin-bottom: 4.5rem;
`

export const HomePage = () => (
  <PageTemplate>
    <Banner />
    <Spacing />
    <RestaurantsSection title="Our favorite picks" />
    <Spacing />
    <AwardWinningSection />
    <Spacing />
    <CategoriesSection categories={categories} />
    <Spacing />
  </PageTemplate>
)

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import { CSSTransition } from 'react-transition-group'
     3→
     4→import { useKey, useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks'
     5→import { Button } from '../Button'
     6→import { Heading } from '../typography'
     7→
     8→import { SidebarContainer, SidebarContent, SidebarFooter, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Sidebar.styles'
     9→
    10→type SidebarProps = {
    11→  isOpen: boolean
    12→  title: string
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→  footer?: React.ReactNode
    15→}
    16→
    17→export const Sidebar: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<SidebarProps>> = ({
    18→  children,
    19→  footer,
    20→  isOpen,
    21→  title,
    22→  onClose,
    23→}) => {
    24→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    25→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    26→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    27→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    28→
    29→  return (
    30→    <>
    31→      <CSSTransition
    32→        in={isOpen}
    33→        timeout={300}
    34→        classNames="sidebar"
    35→        unmountOnExit
    36→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    37→      >
    38→        <SidebarContainer data-testid="sidebar" ref={contentRef}>
    39→          <TopBar>
    40→            <Heading level={4}>{title}</Heading>
    41→            <Button
    42→              aria-label="close sidebar"
    43→              data-testid="sidebar-close-btn"
    44→              onClick={onClose}
    45→              clear
    46→              round
    47→              icon="cross"
    48→              iconSize={16}
    49→            />
    50→          </TopBar>
    51→          <SidebarContent data-testid="sidebar-content">{children}</SidebarContent>
    52→          {footer && <SidebarFooter data-testid="sidebar-footer">{footer}</SidebarFooter>}
    53→        </SidebarContainer>
    54→      </CSSTransition>
    55→
    56→      <CSSTransition
    57→        in={isOpen}
    58→        timeout={300}
    59→        classNames="backdrop"
    60→        unmountOnExit
    61→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    62→      >
    63→        <Backdrop data-testid="Sidebar-backdrop" onClick={onClose} />
    64→      </CSSTransition>
    65→    </>
    66→  )
    67→}
    68→

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import styled, { css } from 'styled-components'
     3→
     4→type FontSize = 'body' | 'bodyS' | 'bodyXS' | 'bodyXXS'
     5→type FontWeight = 'regular' | 'medium' | 'bold' | 'black'
     6→
     7→type StyledBodyProps = {
     8→  $size: string
     9→  $fontWeight: string
    10→}
    11→
    12→const BodyBase = styled.p<StyledBodyProps>(
    13→  ({ $size, $fontWeight, color: textColor, theme: { typography, color } }) => css`
    14→    display: block;
    15→    font-family: 'Hind';
    16→    color: ${textColor || color.primaryText};
    17→    font-weight: ${typography.fontWeight[$fontWeight as FontWeight]};
    18→    font-size: ${typography.fontSize[`body${$size}` as FontSize]};
    19→  `
    20→)
    21→
    22→type AsElement = 'span' | 'p' | 'label' | 'figcaption'
    23→
    24→type ElementProps<T extends AsElement> = T extends 'label'
    25→  ? React.LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>
    26→  : T extends 'span'
    27→    ? React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>
    28→    : T extends 'p'
    29→      ? React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLParagraphElement>
    30→      : React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>
    31→
    32→type DefaultProps = {
    33→  className?: string
    34→  size?: 'S' | 'XS' | 'XXS'
    35→  fontWeight?: 'regular' | 'medium' | 'bold' | 'black'
    36→  type?: AsElement
    37→  color?: string
    38→  children: React.ReactNode | string
    39→}
    40→
    41→type BodyProps = DefaultProps & ElementProps<AsElement>
    42→
    43→export const Body: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<BodyProps>> = ({
    44→  size = '',
    45→  fontWeight = 'regular',
    46→  type = 'p',
    47→  color,
    48→  children,
    49→  className,
    50→  ...props
    51→}) => (
    52→  <BodyBase
    53→    as={type}
    54→    $size={size}
    55→    $fontWeight={fontWeight}
    56→    color={color}
    57→    className={className}
    58→    {...props}
    59→  >
    60→    {children}
    61→  </BodyBase>
    62→)
    63→

import styled, { useTheme } from 'styled-components'

const StyledSVG = styled.svg`
  width: 100px;
  height: 100px;
  position: absolute;
  left: 50%;
  bottom: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
`

export const Spinner = () => {
  const { color } = useTheme()
  return (
    <StyledSVG
      xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
      viewBox="0 0 100 100"
      preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid"
    >
      <text transform="translate(-50 0)" fill={color.primaryText}>
        Looking for some food...
      </text>
      <animate attributeName="opacity" dur="1s" keyTimes="0;0.4;1" values="0;0;1" />
      <g>
        <circle cx="60" cy="50" r="4" fill="#22aca7">
          <animate
            attributeName="cx"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="95;35"
            keyTimes="0;1"
            begin="-0.6566000000000001s"
          />
          <animate
            attributeName="fill-opacity"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="0;1;1"
            keyTimes="0;0.2;1"
            begin="-0.6566000000000001s"
          />
        </circle>
        <circle cx="60" cy="50" r="4" fill="#22aca7">
          <animate
            attributeName="cx"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="95;35"
            keyTimes="0;1"
            begin="-0.3234s"
          />
          <animate
            attributeName="fill-opacity"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="0;1;1"
            keyTimes="0;0.2;1"
            begin="-0.3234s"
          />
        </circle>
        <circle cx="60" cy="50" r="4" fill="#22aca7">
          <animate
            attributeName="cx"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="95;35"
            keyTimes="0;1"
            begin="0s"
          />
          <animate
            attributeName="fill-opacity"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="0;1;1"
            keyTimes="0;0.2;1"
            begin="0s"
          />
        </circle>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(-15 0)">
        <path d="M50 50L20 50A30 30 0 0 0 80 50Z" fill="#61d8de" transform="rotate(90 50 50)" />
        <path d="M50 50L20 50A30 30 0 0 0 80 50Z" fill="#61d8de">
          <animateTransform
            attributeName="transform"
            type="rotate"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="0 50 50;45 50 50;0 50 50"
            keyTimes="0;0.5;1"
          />
        </path>
        <path d="M50 50L20 50A30 30 0 0 1 80 50Z" fill="#61d8de">
          <animateTransform
            attributeName="transform"

content.thinking:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me plan my approach:

## Key analysis summary:

**Provider tree** (from App.tsx):
1. `BrowserRouter` (React Router)
2. `StoreProvider` (Redux with `store`)
3. `ThemeProvider` (styled-components with light/dark theme)
4. `GlobalStyle` (global CSS via styled-components)

**API**: Axios calls to `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants`
- `GET BASE_URL` → list of restaurants
- `GET BASE_URL?id={id}` → single restaurant


- `GET BASE_URL?category={category}` → restaurants by category

I'm noticing something about the mocking setup though. The `api/index.ts` checks for Storybook and Vitest browser mode to disable caching, but it still makes real HTTP requests through axios. There's a mocked version in `conditional-logic.ts`, but that's not what's being imported. Let me trace through the hooks to see which API file they're actually using.

Looking at `src/api/hooks.ts`, they import from `./index`, which means they're using the real API that makes actual HTTP calls. The conditional-logic file appears to be unused, so I'll need MSW to intercept those axios requests in Storybook and Vitest.

For the portal, I need the `#modal` div that's created in Routes.tsx. Dark mode is handled through `use-dark-mode` which manages localStorage or document.body.classList internally. CSS is all handled via styled-components' GlobalStyle component, so there are no root-level CSS imports to worry about.

My approach is to install the MSW Storybook addon and create handlers for the API endpoints like the restaurant category endpoint. I'm refining my component selection for the stories—some of the simpler typography components like Heading and Body won't showcase much, so I'm swapping them out for more complex ones like TopBanner and Header that demonstrate real functionality like Redux integration and dark mode handling, plus the full page components that involve routing and data fetching.

Now I need to set up the provider tree for the Storybook preview—I'll need MemoryRouter for routing, Redux store, styled-components ThemeProvider with the light theme, and GlobalStyle. The key decision is whether to create a fresh store for each story to avoid state leakage or use a reset pattern, but generating a new store via configureStore for each story feels cleaner. I'll create a fresh store in the decorator by importing the `rootReducer` and using `configureStore` to instantiate a new one each time. For the MSW handlers, I need to set up interceptors for the axios requests to the restaurant endpoints, matching the different query parameter patterns for fetching all restaurants, a specific one by ID, or filtering by category.

Writing MSW handler logic... MemoryRouter is available in the current version, so I can use it for Storybook. Since components like Header need Router context for Links to work, I'll wrap everything with MemoryRouter in a global decorator. For pages that need route parameters like RestaurantDetailPage, I'll set up the MemoryRouter with the appropriate initial entries to provide those params.

Now I'm ready to start implementing the setup files and stories. I'll need to configure MSW handlers, update the preview file, and potentially install the MSW Storybook addon if it's not already there.

1→import { useMemo } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import { ShoppingCartItem } from '../ShoppingCartItem/ShoppingCartItem'
     4→import { CartItem } from '../../../app-state/cart'
     5→import { Body } from '../../typography/Body'
     6→import { toCurrency } from '../../../helpers'
     7→
     8→import {
     9→  OrderSummaryContainer,
    10→  BottomContainer,
    11→  CartItemsContainer,
    12→  StyledHeading,
    13→} from './OrderSummary.styles'
    14→
    15→type OrderSummaryProps = {
    16→  cartItems: CartItem[]
    17→}
    18→
    19→export const OrderSummary = ({ cartItems }: OrderSummaryProps) => {
    20→  const totalPrice = useMemo(
    21→    () => cartItems.map((item) => item.quantity * item.price).reduce((acc, next) => acc + next, 0),
    22→    [cartItems]
    23→  )
    24→  return (
    25→    <>
    26→      <OrderSummaryContainer>
    27→        <StyledHeading level={3} $withMargin>
    28→          Your order
    29→        </StyledHeading>
    30→        <CartItemsContainer>
    31→          {cartItems.length > 0 ? (
    32→            cartItems.map((item) => <ShoppingCartItem key={item.id} item={item} />)
    33→          ) : (
    34→            <Body>Your cart is empty.</Body>
    35→          )}
    36→        </CartItemsContainer>
    37→        <BottomContainer>
    38→          <Body>Total</Body>
    39→          <StyledHeading level={2}>{toCurrency(totalPrice)}</StyledHeading>
    40→        </BottomContainer>
    41→      </OrderSummaryContainer>
    42→    </>
    43→  )
    44→}
    45→

Now I have enough info. Let me write all the stories now. I'll write 10 story files:

1. **Button** - with CssCheck, Default, Clear, WithIcon, Disabled
2. **Badge** - Default, CustomText
3. **Review** - VeryGood, NoReviews, Excellent
4. **RestaurantCard** - Default, Closed, New, Loading
5. **Category** - Squared, Rounded
6. **TopBanner** - Default, WithPhoto
7. **OrderSummary** - WithItems, Empty
8. **HomePage** - Default


9. **CategoryListPage** - Default
10. **RestaurantDetailPage** - Default (with route params)

For the CssCheck story on Button, I need to verify the computed styles—checking that the border radius applies correctly based on the round prop, the font family is set to Hind, and the background color switches between the primary dark grey and the clear button variant.

Now I'm ready to write out all the stories, though I need to be careful with the page components since they rely on routing hooks like useParams and useNavigate.

The global MemoryRouter decorator won't provide route parameters to pages that need them. For RestaurantDetailPage which calls `useParams<'id'>()`, I need to wrap it in an actual Route component so the params are available. I'll use a render function in the story itself to set up the Routes/Route structure rather than adding another decorator.

Writing the story setup... I'm realizing the global decorator would still wrap any story-level router, creating nesting issues. The cleanest solution is to move the MemoryRouter out of the global decorator entirely and add it at the meta level for stories that need routing, so I can control exactly which components get wrapped and avoid conflicts with route parameters. that would require every story file to add the router. Let me think about this differently.

Actually, the react-router-dom v7 useParams hook returns empty object if there's no matching route. So `const { id = '' } = useParams<'id'>()` would set id to ''. The RestaurantDetailPage would then try to fetch a restaurant with id='', which would likely return nothing from the MSW handler (since no restaurant has id=''), and the page would render as loading forever, or with an error.

But actually, looking at the MSW handler I wrote, if id is falsy it wouldn't trigger that branch. So with an empty id, the API call would be `BASE_URL?id=` which has an empty searchParams value, and the handler would fall through to the default case and return all restaurants instead.

Let me trace through the actual flow: when id is empty, the useFetchRestaurant hook calls getRestaurantById with an empty string, which constructs a query with `?id=` and sends it to the API.

The MSW handler checks `url.searchParams.get('id')` which returns an empty string (falsy), so it skips the single restaurant branch and returns the full array instead. But the hook expects a single Restaurant object, not an array, which would cause a type mismatch and likely break the component.

So I do need to set up proper routing with params. The solution is to use a decorator at the story level that wraps the component in a Routes element with the correct path pattern, while keeping the global MemoryRouter decorator in place.

Actually, I'm realizing the issue: the global MemoryRouter defaults to `/`, but the Route path `/restaurants/:id` won't match that URL, so the component won't render at all. I think the cleanest approach is to remove MemoryRouter from the global decorator and let each story meta provide its own router setup based on what it needs. Setting up the story-level decorator... Setting up the story metadata with the router initial entries parameter and wrapping the story with Routes to handle the dynamic route parameters. For pages that don't need route params like CategoryListPage, the default route works fine, so I'll focus on writing stories for the pages that actually need them.

Now I'm thinking through which components deserve stories—I'll prioritize the core reusables like Button and Badge, then move to more complex ones like RestaurantCard and Category, plus the full page stories for HomePage and the detail pages.

Before I start writing the story files, I should check what those existing default stories are actually referencing to avoid conflicts or duplication.

All tasks complete. Here's a summary of what was set up:

**Configuration files:**
- `.storybook/preview.tsx` - Full provider tree (MemoryRouter, Redux StoreProvider, styled-components ThemeProvider, GlobalStyle), MSW initialization, configurable route parameters

**10 story files (37 tests total, all passing):**

|---|---|---|

| `OrderSummary.stories.tsx` | WithItems, Empty, SingleItem | Cart data display |

| `CategoryListPage.stories.tsx` | Default | Static page with router |
| `RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx` | Default, WithDesserts, WithDrinks | Page with route params + MSW data |

1→import { useLayoutEffect } from 'react'

    11→export const AppRoutes: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<unknown>> = ({ children }) => {
    12→  const location = useLocation()
    13→  // Scroll to top when a path changes
    14→  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    15→    window.scrollTo(0, 0)
    16→  }, [location.pathname])
    17→
    18→  return (
    19→    <>
    20→      <div id="modal" />
    21→      <Routes>
    22→        <Route path="/categories" element={<CategoryListPage />} />
    23→        <Route path="/categories/:id" element={<CategoryDetailPage />} />
    24→        <Route path="/restaurants/:id" element={<RestaurantDetailPage />} />
    25→        <Route path="/checkout" element={<CheckoutPage />} />
    26→        <Route path="/success" element={<SuccessPage />} />
    27→        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
    28→        {children}
    29→      </Routes>
    30→    </>
    31→  )
    32→}

1→import { createSlice, CaseReducer, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→import { CartItem } from '../cart'
     4→
     5→export type OrderState = {
     6→  items: CartItem[]
     7→}
     8→
     9→const saveOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState, PayloadAction<CartItem[]>> = (state, action) => {
    10→  state.items = action.payload
    11→}
    12→
    13→const clearOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState> = (state) => {
    14→  state.items = []
    15→}
    16→
    17→const initialState: OrderState = {
    18→  items: [],
    19→}
    20→
    21→const orderSlice = createSlice({
    22→  name: 'order',
    23→  initialState,
    24→  reducers: {
    25→    saveOrderAction: saveOrder,
    26→    clearOrderAction: clearOrder,
    27→  },
    28→})
    29→
    30→// Extract the action creators object and the reducer
    31→export const { actions, reducer } = orderSlice
    32→
    33→// Extract and export each action creator by name
    34→export const { saveOrderAction, clearOrderAction } = actions
    35→
    36→// Export the reducer, either as a default or named export
    37→export default reducer
    38→

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}

    11→type ModalProps = {

    15→
    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→export const useKey = (key: string, keyDownCb = () => {}, keyUpCb = () => {}): boolean => {
     4→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false)
     5→  const match = useCallback((event: any) => key.toLowerCase() === event.key.toLowerCase(), [key])
     6→
     7→  const onKeyDown = useCallback(
     8→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
     9→      if (match(event)) {
    10→        setPressed(true)
    11→        keyDownCb()
    12→      }
    13→    },
    14→    [keyDownCb, match]
    15→  )
    16→
    17→  const onKeyUp = useCallback(
    18→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
    19→      if (match(event)) {
    20→        setPressed(false)
    21→        keyUpCb()
    22→      }
    23→    },
    24→    [keyUpCb, match]
    25→  )
    26→
    27→  useEffect(() => {
    28→    globalThis.addEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    29→    globalThis.addEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    30→
    31→    return () => {
    32→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    33→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    34→    }
    35→  }, [key, onKeyDown, onKeyUp])
    36→
    37→  return pressed
    38→}

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me provide a thorough summary of the codebase exploration:

## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis

### 1. Project Structure

This is a **food delivery/restaurant app (MealDrop)** built with React, TypeScript, and Vite. The project has a clear directory organization:

- `/src` - Source code
- `/public` - Static assets (sprite-map.svg, images)
- `.storybook` - Storybook configuration
- `node_modules` - Dependencies

### 2. Entry Point

**Main file: `/src/index.tsx`**
- Uses React 18's `createRoot` API
- Mounts app into `#root` element
- Wraps App component with StrictMode
- Registers service worker (currently unregistered)

### 3. App.tsx & Provider Tree

**File: `/src/App.tsx`**

The application uses a complete provider stack:

```
<StrictMode>
  <Router> (BrowserRouter)
    <StoreProvider> (Redux)
      <ThemeProvider> (styled-components)
        <GlobalStyle />
        <AppRoutes />
      </ThemeProvider>
    </StoreProvider>
  </Router>
</StrictMode>
```

**Providers Used:**
1. **React Router** - `BrowserRouter` for navigation
2. **Redux** - State management via `react-redux` Provider
3. **Styled Components** - `ThemeProvider` for theming
4. **Dark Mode** - `use-dark-mode` hook (controlled at App level)

### 4. All Providers/Contexts

**Redux Store** (`/src/app-state/store.ts`):
- Combines two slices: `cart` and `order`
- Exports typed hooks: `useAppDispatch` and `useAppSelector`

**Redux Slices:**

**Cart Slice** (`/src/app-state/cart/cart.ts`):
- State: `{ visible: boolean, items: CartItem[] }`
- Actions: `toggleVisibilityAction`, `saveItemAction`, `clearItemAction`, `removeItemAction`, `clearCartAction`
- Selectors: `selectCartVisibility`, `selectCartItems`, `selectCartItemsCount`, `selectCartTotal`

**Order Slice** (`/src/app-state/order/order.ts`):
- State: `{ items: CartItem[] }`
- Actions: `saveOrderAction`, `clearOrderAction`
- Selectors: `selectOrderItems`, `selectOrderTotal`

**Dark Mode:**
- Managed by `use-dark-mode` hook at App level
- Reads/writes to `globalThis.window`
- Switches between `lightTheme` and `darkTheme`

### 5. All CSS/Style Imports at Root

**Global Styles** (`/src/styles/GlobalStyle.tsx`):
- Imports: `react-multi-carousel/lib/styles.css`, `react-loading-skeleton/dist/skeleton.css`
- Defines Montserrat (400, 500, 700, 900) and Hind (400, 500) fonts from Google Fonts
- Includes CSS reset via `resetCSS`
- Smooth transitions for dark mode (250ms)
- Global classes: `.container`, `.container-desktop`, `.bolder`, `.copyright`, `.footer-bottom`
- Carousel overrides for react-multi-carousel

**CSS Reset** (`/src/styles/CSSReset.ts`):
- Normalizes form elements
- Resets default styles on buttons, inputs, selects, textareas
- Fixes SVG overflow
- Sets sensible defaults for positioning

**Theme System** (`/src/styles/theme.ts`):
- **Light Theme** - Default
- **Dark Theme** - Full override of colors
- Uses styled-components `DefaultTheme` interface
- Color palette: baseColors with white, black, red, grey, green, blue variants
- Spacing units based on em (0.25em to 5.25em)
- Border radius: xs(4px) to xxl(40px), round(50%)
- Typography: 4 font sizes, 4 font weights
- Box shadows: card, inner, outerBorder

**Breakpoints** (`/src/styles/breakpoints.ts`):
- XS: 400px, S: 640px, M: 768px, L: 1024px, XL: 1440px

### 6. Router Setup & Pages

**Routes File** (`/src/Routes.tsx`):
- Scrolls to top on route change
- Creates `#modal` div for portals
- Routes:
  - `/` → HomePage
  - `/categories` → CategoryListPage
  - `/categories/:id` → CategoryDetailPage
  - `/restaurants/:id` → RestaurantDetailPage
  - `/checkout` → CheckoutPage
  - `/success` → SuccessPage

**Pages:**

1. **HomePage** (`/src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.tsx`)
   - Uses PageTemplate (default type)
   - Components: Banner, RestaurantsSection, AwardWinningSection, CategoriesSection

2. **CategoryListPage** (`/src/pages/CategoryListPage/CategoryListPage.tsx`)
   - Lists all food categories
   - Static data from `/stub/categories.ts`

3. **CategoryDetailPage** (`/src/pages/CategoryDetailPage/CategoryDetailPage.tsx`)
   - Fetches restaurants by category ID
   - Shows loading skeletons during fetch
   - Error handling for empty results

4. **RestaurantDetailPage** (`/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.tsx`)
   - Fetches single restaurant by ID
   - Shows menu items (food, dessert, drinks)
   - Modal for adding items to cart
   - Redux dispatch: `saveItemAction`, `clearItemAction`

5. **CheckoutPage** (`/src/pages/CheckoutPage/CheckoutPage.tsx`)
   - Multi-step form (Contact Details, Delivery Details)
   - Order summary sidebar
   - Uses Redux: `selectCartItems`

6. **SuccessPage** (`/src/pages/SuccessPage/SuccessPage.tsx`)
   - Shows order confirmation
   - Uses Redux: `selectOrderItems`

**PageTemplate** (`/src/templates/PageTemplate.tsx`):
- Types: `default`, `sticky-header`, `basic`
- Default: Header + Container + Footer
- Sticky-header: Sticky Header + Container + Footer
- Basic: Logo-only Header + Container (no footer)

### 7. Portal Usage

**Portal Component** (`/src/components/Portal.tsx`):
- Uses `createPortal` from react-dom
- Takes selector prop to find target DOM element
- Used for modals and sidebars

**Modal Portal Target:**
- Created in Routes.tsx: `<div id="modal" />`
- Used by Modal component via `<Portal selector="#modal">`

**Modal Component** (`/src/components/Modal/Modal.tsx`):
- Uses Portal to render in #modal
- CSSTransition for animations (300ms)
- Keyboard support: Escape key closes
- Body scroll lock when open
- Backdrop click closes

**Sidebar Component** (`/src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.tsx`):
- Similar animation and keyboard support
- Used by ShoppingCartMenu
- No explicit portal (renders inline with backdrop)

### 8. Data Fetching Patterns

**API Layer** (`/src/api/index.ts` and `/src/api/conditional-logic.ts`):

**Base URL:** `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants`

**API Methods:**
- `getRestaurants()` - Fetch all restaurants
- `getRestaurantById(id)` - Fetch single restaurant
- `getRestaurantsByCategory(category)` - Fetch by category

**Technology:** Axios with caching
- In-memory Map cache
- Cache disabled in test/Storybook environments
- Returns typed AxiosResponse

**Data Fetching Hooks** (`/src/api/hooks.ts`):
- `useFetchRestaurants()` - Returns { status, restaurants, retryRequest }
- `useFetchRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)` - Returns { status, restaurants, retryRequest }
- `useFetchRestaurant(id)` - Returns { status, restaurant, retryRequest }

**Status Values:**
- `idle`, `loading`, `success`
- Error status codes: `404`, `500` (error?.response?.status || 500)

**Conditional Logic** (`/src/api/conditional-logic.ts`):
- Mocked API for Storybook and test environments
- Uses data from `/src/stub/restaurants.ts`
- Real API for production

### 9. Browser State Usage

**NO localStorage or sessionStorage found.**

**Browser State Used:**
- **Dark Mode State:** via `use-dark-mode` hook (reads/writes to window object internally)
- **Document manipulation:** 
  - Body scroll lock: `document.body.style.overflow`
  - Portal element selection: `document.querySelector()`
- **Navigation State:** Managed by React Router (useNavigate, useParams)
- **Keyboard Events:** Global event listeners (useKey hook)

### 10. Key Reusable Components

**Layout Components:**
- `PageTemplate` - Page wrapper with header/footer variants
- `Header` + `HeaderComponent` - Top navigation with cart, theme toggle
- `Footer` - Footer with links
- `Sidebar` - Animated sidebar panel
- `Modal` - Centered modal with animations

**Data Display:**
- `RestaurantCard` + `RestaurantCardSkeleton` - Restaurant preview cards
- `Review` - Star rating display
- `Badge` - Small tag badges
- `Category` - Category card

**Forms:**
- `Input` - Text input field
- `Select` - Dropdown select
- `MultiStepForm` - Multi-step checkout form
- `ContactDetails` - Form step 1
- `DeliveryDetails` - Form step 2

**Shopping Cart:**
- `ShoppingCartMenu` - Sidebar menu with cart items
- `ShoppingCartItem` - Individual cart item in menu
- `OrderSummary` - Order summary display
- `FoodItemModal` - Add-to-cart modal for food items

**Typography:**
- `Heading` - Heading component (h1-h4)
- `Body` - Body text component

**Icons & Images:**
- `Icon` - SVG icon from sprite-map.svg
- `Logo` - App logo
- `AnimatedIllustration` - Animated illustrations
- `TopBanner` - Page header banners

**Navigation:**
- `Breadcrumb` - Breadcrumb navigation

**Utilities:**
- `Portal` - Portal to render in different DOM location
- `Button` - Primary CTA button with variants
- `IconButton` - Icon-only button
- `Spinner` - Loading spinner
- `ErrorBlock` - Error message display
- `PageSection` - Section container with title

**Cart Operations** (Redux actions dispatched):
- Save/add item: `saveItemAction(cartItem)`
- Remove item: `removeItemAction(cartItem)`
- Clear item: `clearItemAction(cartItem)`
- Toggle visibility: `toggleVisibilityAction()`
- Save order: `saveOrderAction(cartItems)`
- Clear order: `clearOrderAction()`

**Helper Functions** (`/src/helpers/index.ts`):
- `toCurrency(number)` - Format price with locale currency
- `isMobile()` - Check if user agent is mobile (regex: /Mobi/i)
- `getCurrency()` - Get locale currency code

### 11. Key Files Summary

**State Management:**
- `/src/app-state/store.ts` - Redux store config
- `/src/app-state/cart/cart.ts` - Cart slice
- `/src/app-state/order/order.ts` - Order slice
- `/src/app-state/hooks.ts` - Typed Redux hooks

**Styling:**
- `/src/styles/theme.ts` - Theme definitions
- `/src/styles/GlobalStyle.tsx` - Global styles
- `/src/styles/CSSReset.ts` - CSS reset
- `/src/styles/breakpoints.ts` - Responsive breakpoints

**Components:** 22+ reusable components in `/src/components/`

**Pages:** 6 pages in `/src/pages/`

**Hooks:**
- `/src/hooks/useKeyboard.ts` - Keyboard event listener
- `/src/hooks/useBodyScrollLock.ts` - Body scroll control

**API:**
- `/src/api/index.ts` - API client with axios
- `/src/api/hooks.ts` - Data fetching hooks
- `/src/api/conditional-logic.ts` - Mocked vs real API

**Stub Data:**
- `/src/stub/restaurants.ts` - Mock restaurant data
- `/src/stub/categories.ts` - Food categories
- `/src/stub/cart-items.ts` - Mock cart items

All files are thoroughly type-safe with TypeScript, using styled-components for styling, Redux Toolkit for state management, and React Router for navigation.

     6→import { RestaurantDetailPage } from './pages/RestaurantDetailPage'

    27→        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
    28→        {children}
    29→      </Routes>
    30→    </>
    31→  )
    32→}

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}
   101→

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import { CSSTransition } from 'react-transition-group'
     3→
     4→import { Portal } from '../Portal'
     5→import { useKey } from '../../hooks/useKeyboard'
     6→import { Button } from '../Button'
     7→import { useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks/useBodyScrollLock'
     8→
     9→import { ModalContent, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Modal.styles'
    10→
    11→type ModalProps = {
    12→  isOpen: boolean
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    23→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    24→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    25→
    26→  return (
    27→    <Portal selector="#modal">
    28→      <CSSTransition
    29→        in={isOpen}
    30→        timeout={300}
    31→        classNames="modal"
    32→        unmountOnExit
    33→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    34→      >
    35→        <ModalContent data-testid="modal" ref={contentRef}>
    36→          <TopBar>
    37→            <Button
    38→              data-testid="modal-close-btn"
    39→              onClick={onClose}
    40→              clear
    41→              round
    42→              icon="cross"
    43→              aria-label="close modal"
    44→              iconSize={16}
    45→            />
    46→          </TopBar>
    47→          {children}
    48→        </ModalContent>
    49→      </CSSTransition>
    50→
    51→      <CSSTransition
    52→        in={isOpen}
    53→        timeout={300}
    54→        classNames="backdrop"
    55→        unmountOnExit
    56→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    57→      >
    58→        <Backdrop data-testid="modal-backdrop" ref={backdropRef} onClick={onClose} />
    59→      </CSSTransition>
    60→    </Portal>
    61→  )
    62→}
    63→

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import { CSSTransition } from 'react-transition-group'
     3→
     4→import { useKey, useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks'
     5→import { Button } from '../Button'
     6→import { Heading } from '../typography'
     7→
     8→import { SidebarContainer, SidebarContent, SidebarFooter, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Sidebar.styles'
     9→
    10→type SidebarProps = {
    11→  isOpen: boolean
    12→  title: string
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→  footer?: React.ReactNode
    15→}
    16→
    17→export const Sidebar: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<SidebarProps>> = ({
    18→  children,
    19→  footer,
    20→  isOpen,
    21→  title,
    22→  onClose,
    23→}) => {
    24→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    25→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    26→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    27→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    28→
    29→  return (
    30→    <>
    31→      <CSSTransition
    32→        in={isOpen}
    33→        timeout={300}
    34→        classNames="sidebar"
    35→        unmountOnExit
    36→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    37→      >
    38→        <SidebarContainer data-testid="sidebar" ref={contentRef}>
    39→          <TopBar>
    40→            <Heading level={4}>{title}</Heading>
    41→            <Button
    42→              aria-label="close sidebar"
    43→              data-testid="sidebar-close-btn"
    44→              onClick={onClose}
    45→              clear
    46→              round
    47→              icon="cross"
    48→              iconSize={16}
    49→            />
    50→          </TopBar>
    51→          <SidebarContent data-testid="sidebar-content">{children}</SidebarContent>
    52→          {footer && <SidebarFooter data-testid="sidebar-footer">{footer}</SidebarFooter>}
    53→        </SidebarContainer>
    54→      </CSSTransition>
    55→
    56→      <CSSTransition
    57→        in={isOpen}
    58→        timeout={300}
    59→        classNames="backdrop"
    60→        unmountOnExit
    61→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    62→      >
    63→        <Backdrop data-testid="Sidebar-backdrop" onClick={onClose} />
    64→      </CSSTransition>
    65→    </>
    66→  )
    67→}
    68→

    19→  switch (type) {

    42→      <Container>{children}</Container>
